mechanism_narrative: With FEDFUNDS pinned near the zero lower bound (0.13% as of August 2012), the discount rate applied to equity cash flows remains structurally compressed, sustaining elevated equity valuations and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as long as the policy rate stays near zero.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.13% in August 2012, near zero lower bound since Dec 2008
failure_condition: 20-day forward SPX returns during periods with FEDFUNDS < 0.25% are not significantly higher than the full-sample average (t-test p > 0.05), or rank IC falls below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in UNRATE of at least 0.2 percentage points over a trailing 3-month window signals improving labor market conditions and consumer income prospects, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent quarter as earnings expectations are revised upward.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE falls by >= 0.2pp over trailing 3 months
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 8.1% in August 2012, down from 8.3% in prior months
failure_condition: Triggered instances of UNRATE decline show 60-day forward SPX positive-return frequency not exceeding the unconditional base rate (65.5%) by a statistically significant margin, or rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Joint acceleration in RSAFS (retail sales) and HOUST (housing starts) indicates a synchronized consumer and housing demand recovery that is not captured by either series alone; when both series show positive month-over-month growth simultaneously, forward-looking growth expectations rise and S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days are positive.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 746k units July 2012 vintage, up from prior year lows near 500k
failure_condition: When both RSAFS and HOUST show simultaneous positive monthly growth, 20-day forward SPX returns are not significantly higher than periods where only one or neither series is positive (p > 0.05), or interaction term adds no incremental rank IC versus univariate proxies.

mechanism_narrative: In an early-cycle recovery regime characterized by below-trend capacity utilization, positive INDPRO growth signals expanding industrial output and reflects genuine supply-side recovery, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter; in a late-cycle or overheating regime, the same INDPRO growth signal instead precedes margin compression and does not predict positive returns.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 96.82 (2017=100) August 2012 vintage, consistent with sub-trend post-recession output level
failure_condition: 60-day forward SPX returns conditioned on positive INDPRO growth during early-cycle regimes are not significantly different from returns conditioned on positive INDPRO growth during late-cycle regimes, indicating the regime split does not matter; or rank IC < 0.03 in early-cycle subsample.

mechanism_narrative: Positive month-over-month growth in PAYEMS reflects ongoing labor market expansion that supports short-term risk appetite, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as markets react to the incremental payroll print.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 133,300 thousand persons August 2012 vintage, trending upward through 2012
failure_condition: 5-day forward SPX returns following positive PAYEMS monthly prints are not significantly different from returns following flat or negative prints (t-test p > 0.05), or rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL year-over-year inflation crosses above 3%, markets price in an increased probability of monetary tightening, raising discount rates and compressing equity valuations, which produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 229.918 (revised) August 2012, YoY inflation below 2% at decision time
failure_condition: 20-day forward SPX returns during periods when CPIAUCSL YoY > 3% are not significantly lower than baseline unconditional returns (p > 0.05), or the trigger condition is never activated during the walk-forward test window, invalidating testability.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous positive growth in GDPC1 (real output) and RSAFS (retail sales) confirms a broad-based demand expansion that is more predictive of sustained equity gains than either series alone, because it distinguishes durable consumption-driven growth from transitory output fluctuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 406,748 million August 2012 vintage showing continued retail growth
failure_condition: The joint positive-growth signal from GDPC1 and RSAFS does not produce 60-day forward SPX returns significantly higher than either single-series positive-growth signal alone (p > 0.05), or interaction term rank IC is not incremental over univariate proxies.

mechanism_narrative: During a zero-lower-bound quantitative easing regime, rising HOUST housing starts amplify the equity-positive impact of accommodative FEDFUNDS policy because housing recovery is a key transmission channel targeted by QE, producing outsized positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days when both conditions hold jointly compared to either alone.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS < 0.25% and HOUST rising for 2 consecutive months
evidence_refs: fact:QE3 announced September 13 2012 explicitly targeting mortgage-backed securities to support housing
regime_hint: zero lower bound quantitative easing regime
failure_condition: 20-day forward SPX returns when both FEDFUNDS is near zero and HOUST is rising are not significantly higher than returns when only FEDFUNDS is near zero without rising HOUST (p > 0.05), or the joint trigger produces fewer positive-return instances than the unconditional base rate of 65.5%.
